## Update Customer Product List

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:patch-customer-shopper-customers-organizations-customers-product-lists-by-version-organization-id-customer-id-list-id>`

Changes a product list. Changeable properties are the name, description, and if the list is public.
This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/customer/shopper-customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customers/{customerId}/product-lists/{listId}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>version</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Customer Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The customer ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>List Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The product list ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Customer Auth Config](#CustomerAuthConfig)

### Throws

- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:BAD_REQUEST
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CLIENT_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CONNECTIVITY
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_FOUND
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TIMEOUT
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:UNAUTHORIZED
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Customer Product List Item

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:patch-customer-shopper-customers-organizations-customers-product-lists-items-by-version-organization-id-customer-id-list-id-item-id>`

Updates an item of a customerâs product list. Considered values from the request body are: priority:
This is the priority of the customerâs product list item. public: This is the flag whether the
customerâs product list item is public. quantity: This is the quantity of the customerâs product
list item. Used for product item type only. custom properties in the form c\_<CUSTOM_NAME>: The
custom property must correspond to a custom attribute (<CUSTOM_NAME>) defined for ProductListItem.
The value of this property must be valid for the type of custom attribute defined for
ProductListItem. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/customer/shopper-customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customers/{customerId}/product-lists/{listId}/items/{itemId}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>List Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The product list ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Customer Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The customer ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>version</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Item Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the product list item to update.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update Billing Address For Basket

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:update-checkout-shopper-baskets-organizations-baskets-billing-address-by-version-organization-id-basket-id>`

Sets the billing address of a basket. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/checkout/shopper-baskets/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/baskets/{basketId}/billing-address
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>version</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Basket Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the basket to be modified.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use As Shipping</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use As Shipping</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Update Customer For Basket

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:update-checkout-shopper-baskets-organizations-baskets-customer-by-version-organization-id-basket-id>`

Sets customer information for an existing basket. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/checkout/shopper-baskets/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/baskets/{basketId}/customer
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>version</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Basket Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the basket to be modified.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Update Shipping Address For Shipment

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:update-checkout-shopper-baskets-organizations-baskets-shipments-shipping-address-by-version-organization-id-basket-id-shipment-id>`

Sets a shipping address of a specific shipment of a basket. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request
to the
/checkout/shopper-baskets/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/baskets/{basketId}/shipments/{shipmentId}/shipping-address
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>version</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Basket Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the basket to be modified.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shipment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the shipment to be modified.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use As Billing</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use As Billing</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update Customer Password

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:update-customer-shopper-customers-organizations-customers-password-by-version-organization-id-customer-id>`

Updates the customer''s password. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/customer/shopper-customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customers/{customerId}/password
endpoint.

### Parameters

## Authorize Customer

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:create-customer-shopper-customers-organizations-customers-actions-login-by-version-organization-id>`

Obtains a new JSON Web Token (JWT)for a guest or registered customer. Tokens are returned as an HTTP
Authorization:Bearer response header entry. These kinds of request are supported, as specified by
the type: Type guest - creates a guest (non-authenticated) customer and returns a token for the
customer. Request Body for guest : {"type": "guest"} Type credentials - authenticates credentials
passed in the HTTP Authorization:Basic request header, returning a token for a successfully
authenticated customer, otherwise it throws an AuthenticationFailedException. Request Body for guest
: {"type": "credentials"} Type refresh - examines the token passed in the HTTP Authorization:Bearer
request header and when valid returns a new token with an updated expiry time. Request Body for
guest : {"type": "refresh"} For a request of type credentials: Updates profile attributes for the
customer (for example, "last-visited"). Handles the maximum number of failed login attempts. About
JWT The token contains 3 sections: The header section (specifies token type and algorithm used), the
payload section (contains customer information, client ID, issue, and expiration time), finally the
signature section records the token signature. A token is created and returned to the client
whenever a registered customer logs in (type "credentials") or a guest customer requests it (type
"guest"). The token is returned in the response header as Authorization: Bearer --token-- The client
has to include the token in the request header as Authorization: Bearer --token-- in any follow-up
request. The server declines any follow-up requests without a token or which cannot be verified
based on the token signature or expiration time. A token nearing its expiration time should be
exchanged for a new one (type "refresh"). See "API Usage > JWT" for more details on using JWT as an
authentication mechanism. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/customer/shopper-customers/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/customers/actions/login
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>version</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Client ID for application identification</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Site Id</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Authorization:Basic for type credentials Authorization:Bearer for type refresh</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CLIENT_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CONNECTIVITY
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_FOUND
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TIMEOUT
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:UNAUTHORIZED
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Authenticate Customer

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:create-shopper-auth-organizations-oauth2-login-by-version-organization-id>`

Authenticates an ECOM customer. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/shopper/auth/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/oauth2/login endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base64-encoded username and password for HTTP Basic authentication.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Shopper Token Config](#ShopperTokenConfig)

### Throws

- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:BAD_REQUEST
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CLIENT_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CONNECTIVITY
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_FOUND
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TIMEOUT
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:UNAUTHORIZED
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Access Token

`<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:create-shopper-auth-organizations-oauth2-token-by-version-organization-id>`

Gets a JWT access token and a refresh token for the shopper or guest. This is the second step of the
OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. The client appplication gets an access token through the back
channel (a trusted server) by passing in the client credentials and the authorization code retrieved
from the `authorize` endpoint.

When refreshing the access token with a private client ID, the refresh token is not regenerated.
However, when refreshing the access token with a public client ID, the refresh token is regenerated.
The old refresh token is voided with every refresh call, so the refresh token must be replaced to
store the new refresh token.

This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/shopper/auth/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/oauth2/token endpoint.

> [!IMPORTANT] Use the `channel_id` query parameter because it will be required in the future.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Organization Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Organization ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base64-encoded string for HTTP Basic authentication. The string is composed of a client ID and a client secret separated by a colon, for example: <code>clientId:clientSecret</code>. This field is required unless the grant type is <code>authorization_code_pkce</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Shopper Token Config](#ShopperTokenConfig)

### Throws

- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:BAD_REQUEST
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CLIENT_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:CONNECTIVITY
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:NOT_FOUND

- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- COMMERCE-CLOUD-SHOPPER-API:TIMEOUT

### Default Query Param

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Non Proxy Hosts</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tls

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">Crl File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The reconnection strategy to use</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Http Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Status Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason Phrase</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of memory that will be allocated in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded by according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much will be buffer size by expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower will mean that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector 2.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector Reference](shop-api-connector-reference).

## Before You Begin

To avoid dependency errors, update your Maven `settings.xml` file if needed. See
[Maven Reference](../../mule-runtime/latest/maven-reference) for more information (Ignore if already
done).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Shop APIs (B2C Shop Connector) to your Mule
project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs namespace and schema location
and add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `commerce cloud b2c shop` in the search field.
3.  Click Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP listener, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of HTTP **Listener** in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector. For TLS
    configuration information, see [TLS Configuration](../../mule-runtime/latest/tls-configuration).
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy. For Reconnection strategy details, see
    [Reconnection Strategies](../../mule-runtime/latest/reconnection-strategy-about).
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for B2C Shop Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector** and then select the
    desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

    ![Example of operation dragged to flow](_images/shop-connector-add-operation-to-flow.png)

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials that the connector requires to access the target Commerce Cloud B2C Shop
Connector system. Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector supports two different types of connection
provider:

1.  Customer Auth Connection Provider
2.  OAuth2 Application Connection Provider

To configure the global element for Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, configure the connection:
    - [Customer Auth Connection Provider](#ShopConfiguration_CustomersAuth)
    - [OAuth2 Application Connection Provider](#ShopConfiguration_Oauth2Application)

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

\+ You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
(recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to configure
them, see
[Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

\+ . On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
strategy.

1.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
2.  Click **OK**.

### Get the Shopper JWT Token

To get the Shopper JWT Token to use for the
[**Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector Customer Auth Config**](#ShopConfiguration_CustomersAuth):

1.  On the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen, select **Commerce Cloud B2C
    Shop Connector Shopper Token** and click **OK**.
2.  Configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field Name</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Base URI</p></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own base URI.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    ![Shopper token global elements properties config window on General tab](_images/shop-connector-shopper-token-config.jpg)

The XML configuration looks like this:

```xml
    <commerce-cloud-shopper-api:shopper-token-config name="Commerce_Cloud_B2C_Shop_Connector_Shopper_token" doc:name="Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector Shopper token" doc:id="9edebcff-2328-4312-88eb-d8ab4dcbd7cd" >
        <commerce-cloud-shopper-api:shopper-token-connection baseUri="${shopper.baseUri}" />
    </commerce-cloud-shopper-api:shopper-token-config>
```

### Customer Auth Connection Provider Configuration

Before you configure the connection for **Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector Customer Auth Config**,
[get the Shopper JWT Token](#get-shopper-jwt-token).

1.  On the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen, select **Commerce Cloud B2C
    Shop Connector Customer Auth Config** and click **OK**.
2.  Configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field Name</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Base URI</p></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant has its own base URI.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization</p></td><td><p>Provide the Commerce Cloud B2C Shopper JWT Token as the authorization parameter.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    This example adds the base URI to the application properties file and references it using
    placeholders:

    ![Auth config global elements properties config window on General tab](_images/shop-connector-customer-auth-config.jpg)

3.  Click **Test Connection** to verify the configuration.

The XML configuration looks like this:

```xml
    <commerce-cloud-shopper-api:customer-auth-config name="Commerce_Cloud_Shopper_Connector_Customer_auth_config" doc:name="Commerce Cloud Shopper Connector Customer auth config" doc:id="3513fbf9-98ad-45a0-bd8e-5f01f1f340b2">
        <commerce-cloud-shopper-api:shopper-connection baseUri="${shopper.baseUri}" authorization="#[attributes.headers.Authorization]" />
    </commerce-cloud-shopper-api:customer-auth-config>
```

### OAuth2 Application Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

1.  On the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen, select **Commerce Cloud B2C
    Shop Connector Config** and click **OK**.
2.  Configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field Name</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Base Uri</p></td><td><p>Access API URL</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><p>ID of the client account created with Salesforce Commerce Cloud</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><p>Secret of the client account created with Salesforce Commerce Cloud</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token url</p></td><td><p>Access token URL</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><p>Scopes for access</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    ![Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector Config with values for the OAuth 2.0 client credentials connection](_images/shop-connector-oauth2-config.jpg)

    This example adds credentials to the application properties file and references it using
    placeholders:

3.  Click **Test Connection** to verify the configuration.

The XML code for the OAuth 2.0 application connection provider configuration looks like this:

```xml
   <commerce-cloud-shopper-api:config name="Commerce_Cloud_B2C_Shop_Connector_Config" doc:name="Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector Config" doc:id="b487e17a-b80a-4cf4-a2f3-d24d47690844" >
		<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:am-oauth2-connection baseUri="${shopper.baseUri}" >
			<commerce-cloud-shopper-api:oauth-client-credentials clientId="${shopper.clientId}" clientSecret="${shopper.clientSecret}" tokenUrl="${shopper.tokenUrl}" scopes="${shopper.scopes}" />
		</commerce-cloud-shopper-api:am-oauth2-connection>
	</commerce-cloud-shopper-api:config>
```

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## Configure the Other Connector Fields

After you configure a global element and connection information, configure the other fields for the
connector.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Connector Reference](shop-api-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector 2.1 - Troubleshoot

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

There are a few scenarios when the Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector might return 4XX errors.

**400 Bad Request**

Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector might return a `400 Bad Request` error response if the URI
parameters, such as Organization Id, Site Id, and Version parameters are not passed with the proper
values, for example:

- The parameters are passed with the wrong values.
- The parameters are passed with an extra space.

## Solution

Correct and pass the proper values.

**401 Unauthorized**

Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector might return a `401 Unauthorized` error response in the following
cases:

- The Client Id used in the configuration is incorrect.
- The Client Secret used in the configuration is incorrect.

## Solutions

- Correct and pass the proper values for the Client ID and Client Secret.
- If the Client Id and Client Secret are both correct and you are still getting the error, contact
  the Commerce Cloud team.

**403 Forbidden**

Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector might return a `403 Forbidden` error response if the Scopes used
in the configuration are incorrect.

## Solution

- Correct and pass the proper values for the Scopes.

**405 Method Not Allowed**

Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector method returns a `405 Method Not Allowed` error when a resource
that previously supported an HTTP POST got removed and had its HTTP GET method deprecated because of
business decisions.

## Solution

- Contact the Commerce Cloud team.

**415 Unsupported Media Type**

Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector might return a `415 unsupported media type` error response in
these cases:

- The body type the user is passing to the connector `Content` parameter does not match the required
  input type.
- Expression mode is enabled and the output type of the body is not defined as `application/json`.

## Solution

- Use the correct type for the body.
- Enable Expression mode and define the output type of the body as `application/json`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector to Version 2.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Commerce Cloud B2C Shop APIs (B2C Shop Connector) to take
advantage of the functionality provided by the Shopper Customer APIs version 0.0.24.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>1.x.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>2.x.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

- The connector is updated with the latest Salesforce Commerce B2C Shop APIs.
- The Shopper Customer endpoints that did not work in the previous version of the connector are
  fixed.

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, you must create a backup of your files, data, and configurations in
case you need to restore to them.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `commerce cloud b2c shop` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, select **<connector>** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the <connector> dependency version is <latest-version> in the `pom.xml` file in the
    Mule project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

### Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Verify that there are no problems in the project `pom.xml` file.
3.  Test the connection to verify that the operations work.

## Troubleshoot

If there are common known issues and errors that occur when upgrading, give troubleshooting tips.

### Revert the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of B2C Shop Connector, change the
`connector-name-dependency` dependency version in the projectâs `pom.xml` file to the previous
version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` file in Anypoint Studio.
---

# Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector 2.1 - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio.

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-shopper-api
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-shopper-api/current/mule-commerce-cloud-shopper-api.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
      xmlns:commerce-cloud-shopper-api="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-shopper-api"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
      xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
      xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
      xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
      http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
      http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-shopper-api
      http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/commerce-cloud-shopper-api/current/mule-commerce-cloud-shopper-api.xsd
      http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
      http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">
</mule>
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>commerce-cloud-shopperapi</artifactId>
    <version>x.x.x</version>
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace the contents of the `<version>` tag with the version that corresponds to the connector you
are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `Commerce Cloud B2C Shop Connector`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](shop-api-connector-examples).

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
